metrics.narrative: The buyer's business problem is replacing Coupa, confirmed by the 6 Jul 2026 Next Step ("the Coupa replace"). Pain was quantified operationally in the 20 Apr 2026 demo: business users manually gather requirements via email/static forms, procurement re-keys data into sourcing events, and invoice processing runs through email with manual-match exceptions (Avoma notes, 20 Apr 2026). Zycus offered vendor-stated efficiency claims in that session (Merlin Intake configured in 2 hours; ~40% reduction in sourcing-event build time), but no buyer-confirmed dollar value, savings target, or ROI threshold exists against the $225,000 opportunity amount.

champion.narrative: Michelle (Director of Procurement) is the most consistently engaged buyer contact and proactively emailed Zycus on 6 Jul 2026 confirming finalist status and outlining next steps, a meaningful positive signal. There is no evidence yet of her advocating internally to Ian Rosenblum or briefing Zycus on the competing finalist, so she reads as a developing champion, not a confirmed one with demonstrated access to power.

competition.narrative: Salesforce lists Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba as competitors; the 6 Jul 2026 Next Step confirms Coupa is the incumbent being replaced ("the Coupa replace") and that Zycus has been narrowed to a final two. The identity of the second finalist is not named anywhere in the record, leaving a real gap in competitive intelligence for the final stretch.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well-documented and multi-sourced: the 20 Apr 2026 demo recorded manual requirement-gathering, manual re-keying from requisitions into sourcing events, manual contract creation post-award, and manual invoice-matching exceptions (Avoma notes, 20 Apr 2026). The 6 Jul 2026 Next Step confirms the compelling event: an active, tight-timeline replacement of Coupa.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, legal review, or InfoSec activity has surfaced in any call, task, or Next Step through 6 Jul 2026. This is expected at Shortlisted stage (paper process typically begins post-Vendor-Selected per the Zycus contracting motion) but remains entirely undocumented for this deal.

economic_buyer.narrative: Ian Rosenblum, Chief Information Officer, is on the Salesforce contact roles and attended the 2 Apr 2026 and 13 Apr 2026 demos, establishing him as the identified economic buyer for the IT side of the decision. No call, task, or Next Step shows him directly engaged in pricing or commercial discussion; the 6 Jul 2026 Next Step routes pricing breakdowns as a pending next step through Procurement (Michelle, Director of Procurement), not IT.

decision_process.narrative: Authority is split between Procurement (Regina Oliver, Head of Procurement Operations, and Michelle, Director of Procurement) and IT (Ian Rosenblum, CIO, currently mid-handoff from Jason, the outgoing project lead, to an unnamed incoming IT leader per the 6 Jul 2026 Next Step). No document, call, or task maps a sign-off sequence connecting these two tracks.

decision_criteria.narrative: The 20 Apr 2026 demo surfaced concrete, buyer-voiced criteria: native NetSuite integration for master data/contracts/POs/invoices, contract look-up depth (whether a search pulls the master agreement plus every SOW/amendment), SOX/audit-log compliance, and requisition budget-checking (Avoma notes, 20 Apr 2026). No single consolidated Business Requirements Document or weighted scoring rubric is visible in the record, so criteria are real but not fully consolidated.

engagement.top_event: Answer detailed NetSuite integration questions raised by Jessica Craft on the 20 Apr 2026 demo call and propose follow-up sessions if needed.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is buyer-confirmed as a top-two finalist (6 Jul 2026) replacing Coupa, a genuinely strong signal, but the deal carries real structural gaps at Shortlisted stage: the economic buyer isn't yet in commercial conversations, the second finalist is unnamed, the approval chain is unmapped, and three next-step milestones (demo, pricing, implementation) are unscheduled with only 20 days to the recorded close date.

deal_risk.text: Tight, dependency-gated timeline — 20 days to the 29 Jul 2026 close date, with demo, pricing, and implementation discussions unscheduled and gated on the NetSuite decision and IT leadership handoff.
Unidentified competing finalist — the final competitive pitch cannot be tailored without knowing which vendor Zycus is up against.
Paper process entirely undocumented — no MSA, SOW, legal, or InfoSec activity has surfaced yet, and this typically requires real lead time once it begins.
No quantified budget or ROI threshold is on record against the $225,000 opportunity amount.

win_position.text: Named a top-two finalist — Michelle (Director of Procurement) told Zycus by email on 6 Jul 2026 that Zycus has been selected as one of two finalists for the Source-to-Pay transformation project.
Incumbent explicitly being replaced — the 6 Jul 2026 Next Step ties the tight timeline directly to "the Coupa replace," confirming Coupa is being displaced, not a live re-selection threat.
Economic buyer not yet in commercial talks — Ian Rosenblum (CIO) attended demos on 2 Apr and 13 Apr 2026 but has no recorded engagement in pricing or commercial discussion as of 6 Jul 2026.
Second finalist unidentified — the buyer named Zycus one of two finalists on 6 Jul 2026 without naming the other vendor, leaving a blind spot in competitive positioning.

No documented approval chain — decision authority splits between Procurement (Regina Oliver / Michelle) and IT (Ian Rosenblum plus an incoming, unnamed IT leader replacing Jason), with no mapped sign-off sequence as of 6 Jul 2026.

deal_momentum.text: Buyer-initiated finalist confirmation — Michelle emailed the update unprompted on 6 Jul 2026, a genuine two-way buyer touch, not a rep chase.
Two-way email engagement on 29 May 2026 (41 days before this sweep) shows the relationship staying live between formal meetings.
Next milestones unscheduled — the additional demo, pricing breakdown, and implementation discussion flagged on 6 Jul 2026 have no dates attached, with only 20 days left to the 29 Jul 2026 close.

Last actual meeting was roughly 7.5 weeks ago — the 18 May 2026 on-site follow-up demo is the most recent session on record, and its captured content was largely a notetaker/recording-permission discussion.

Two external dependencies gate the next step — Roivant's internal NetSuite platform decision and the Jason-to-new-IT-leader knowledge transfer both sit outside Zycus's control per the 6 Jul 2026 Next Step.

customer_commitment.text: Buyer proactively communicated selection status on 6 Jul 2026 rather than waiting to be asked, a positive commitment signal from Michelle.
Regina Oliver, Peter Knob, and Mark Turon show recent Salesforce task activity as of the 1 Jul 2026 LastActivityDate, indicating a still-engaged, multi-threaded buying group.

No confirmed champion advocacy to the economic buyer has surfaced; Michelle's engagement, while positive, has not yet been shown to extend to internally selling Zycus to Ian Rosenblum or the incoming IT leader.
